text.title: Solo Bug Triage Rubric — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every solo triage pass must enforce. These are HARD rules — sympathy for a vocal reporter cannot override them.

rule.statement: Each bug MUST be labelled with severity ∈ {P0, P1, P2} using the solo ladder: P0 = paying customer cannot use core flow; P1 = paying customer experiences degraded core flow OR ≥10% of total users blocked on a non-core flow; P2 = everything else.

rationale.source: Revenue-honest prioritisation (Levels.fyi / Indie Hackers founder surveys)

rule.rationale: A team-grade ladder over-elevates non-paying noise and starves the work that protects MRR; the solo ladder makes the trade-off explicit.

rule.statement: Reach MUST be captured as a concrete count ("3 paying customers, 47 free users") not a verbal estimate ("a few users"); unknown reach is treated as P2 until measured.
rule.rationale: Verbal estimates inflate by 3–5x under emotional pressure; the count is non-negotiable to keep triage honest.

rule.statement: P0 bugs MUST be fixed before any feature work resumes; P1 bugs MUST be scheduled within the current week; P2 bugs MUST be either scheduled within 90 days OR killed with a one-line `wontfix` rationale.
rule.rationale: Backlogs that grow without expiry rot into unactionable noise and erode trust in the tracker as a planning tool.

rule.statement: Every triage decision MUST record: severity, reach_count (paying + free split), assigned_week_iso OR `wontfix`, and a one-line rationale; bugs without all four fields are blocked from leaving triage.
rule.rationale: Solo founders re-debate the same bug repeatedly when the rationale is missing; the four-field record kills repeat-debate.

rule.statement: The Friday triage block MUST be capped at 60 minutes; if the queue is not cleared in 60 minutes, the unprocessed bugs default to P2 and stay in the backlog — the founder does not extend the block.
rule.rationale: Triage that runs long crowds out building; the cap is what makes the practice sustainable.

example.title: Anchored triage entry
example.code: bug_id: BUG-482 title: "Export-to-CSV fails for orgs with > 5k rows" severity: P1 reach: paying: 4 free: 11 assigned_week_iso: 2026-W21 rationale: "4 paying orgs blocked on a paid-only feature; in-week fix protects renewal."
example.note: Clear severity per the ladder, concrete reach with split, scheduled, rationale.

example.title: Un-anchored triage entry
example.code: "Export thing is broken for some people, P1 I guess, fix soon."
example.note: No reach, no split, no week, no rationale — guaranteed to be re-debated next Friday.
